content.verdict: Curtain Bluff is Antigua's most loyalty-driven luxury resort — a 60-year-old Caribbean classic founded in 1962, perched on a bluff between two beaches (one calm, one breezy) on the south coast. Family-owned, all-inclusive, and famous for the highest repeat-visit rate in the Caribbean (over 50% of guests return), it offers a courtly, multi-generational atmosphere of tennis, sailing, and grand-tradition Caribbean hospitality.
content.best_room: The Morris Bay Junior Suites and One-Bedroom Suites face the calm, swimming-friendly Morris Bay beach with private terraces. The Bluff Suites at the elevated tip of the headland command 270-degree ocean views from both calm and Atlantic-side waters. The Grace Bay Suites on the windward side are quieter but with more dramatic surf. For honeymoons, a Bluff Suite delivers the most romantic perch.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Bluff Welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from V.C. Bird International (35 minutes to Old Road on Antigua's south coast). Curtain Bluff's all-inclusive includes premium spirits, fine wines, and water sports — the welcome drink and orientation tour set the tone. First dinner at the resort's main restaurant, where the dress code in the evening is jacket-required for men in high season.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Two Beaches & Tennis
itinerary_7_nights.description: The bluff position gives Curtain Bluff two distinct beaches: calm, swimmer-friendly Morris Bay on the leeward side, and the breezy Atlantic-fed bay on the windward side. Morning swim at Morris Bay, then a tennis lesson on one of the resort's four courts (Curtain Bluff has hosted the Antigua Tennis Week for decades).

itinerary_7_nights.title: Nelson's Dockyard & English Harbour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Curtain Bluff is 20 minutes from Nelson's Dockyard, the UNESCO World Heritage Georgian naval dockyard. Spend the morning among the restored 18th-century buildings, lunch at the harbourside, and afternoon at the small museum before returning to the resort.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Shirley Heights Sunset
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive up to Shirley Heights, the historic military lookout above English Harbour. The Sunday-evening sunset gathering is Antigua's most famous social event — steel band, barbecue, rum punch, and the panorama down to the yachts in Falmouth Harbour. Even on non-Sunday visits the view is unmatched.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sailing & Snorkelling
itinerary_7_nights.description: Curtain Bluff's water-sports programme is genuinely strong — Hobie Cats, paddleboards, kayaks, snorkel gear, and scuba diving are all included. A morning sail along the south coast and an afternoon snorkel at Cades Reef, Antigua's barrier reef, with vivid corals and clear water.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Stingray City & 365 Beaches
itinerary_7_nights.description: An excursion to Stingray City off the north coast — a shallow sandbar where southern stingrays glide in waist-deep water. On the return, stop at one or two of Antigua's 365 beaches — Half Moon Bay, Ffryes Bay, or Pink Sand Beach — for a beach-bar lunch.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Bluff Morning & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Last swim at Morris Bay, breakfast on the suite terrace, and a relaxed road transfer back to V.C. Bird International. Curtain Bluff's seasoned staff handle every checkout detail with characteristic precision.

caveats: Curtain Bluff is family-friendly and multi-generational — the atmosphere is more grand-traditional Caribbean resort than adults-only honeymoon retreat.
Evening dress code in high season (jackets required for men in the main dining room) is more formal than most Caribbean resorts — appealing to some honeymooners, off-putting to others.
The resort's loyal repeat clientele creates a tight-knit atmosphere that can feel slightly insular to first-time guests.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Curtain Bluff is a strong honeymoon choice for couples who appreciate classic Caribbean tradition, exceptional service, and the security of a 60-year-old all-inclusive operation. The Bluff Suites with panoramic ocean views and the resort's two-beach geography are particular romantic strengths.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: December through April is high season — dry, sunny, and breezy. February through April is peak tennis week. May, June, and November offer lower rates with similar weather. The resort traditionally closes August-October for low season and refurbishment.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: Curtain Bluff has the highest repeat-guest rate in the Caribbean — peak weeks book out 9-12 months ahead. Bluff Suites and Morris Bay Suites sell out first. Shoulder season offers better availability with 4-6 months lead time.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: Curtain Bluff is not adults-only — it has a kids' club and welcomes families, especially during school holidays. The atmosphere is more multi-generational than family-frenetic. Adults-only alternatives include Hermitage Bay, Galley Bay, and Cocobay.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Bluff Suites at the elevated tip of the headland offer the most romantic position with 270-degree ocean views. Morris Bay Suites on the calm leeward beach offer immediate beach access for swimmers.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to V.C. Bird International Airport (ANU). Curtain Bluff is 35 minutes by road through Antigua's interior to the south coast at Old Road — the resort arranges private transfers. Direct flights serve Antigua from London, New York, Miami, Atlanta, and Toronto.
